channel.title: Comments for page "Fear Not"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Fear Not"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's word art, and I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't get it; I didn't think that hard about it. Poetry-ness and word-having is alright. I thought it neat that there were links to Wikipedia, but it otherwise feels forced to make scientific sense of something people probably could on their own. Or maybe I'm the one that doesn't get it. I think it's a neat take on cross-testing/skip interaction (assuming that is what it is) but I'm not big enough a fan of abstract poetry to get or give much to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If that was the intention, the subject material is an afterthought at best. Nothing of the apparent source articles is coming through in this, and we're left with something where the reader is almost wholly responsible for investing it with meaning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wouldn't say it's completely without meaning. The left justified verses have a running theme of there being nothing in the darkness, drawing parallels to 1485's normalized universe. The right justified verses mention connections that the worlds are worse off for, ending with the breaking of a pact and the death and dissolution of the people.

"Nothing there.
Thing here." Anomalous and non-Anomalous worlds meet.

Nothing wrong with making order from chaos, although the author does die sometimes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's no there there. You can't empty the box of Legos on the floor and call it a finished product. Well, I guess you can, but it's not going to mean a whole lot. Poetry without personal weight behind it is grating, and this is no exception. The shoehorning in of links to the wiki seems exceptionally cynical and cheap.

Also, no, I did not write this. Thanks for confusing this issue for the newer folks, guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have no idea what the fuck is going on here.

I like it. +1

item.title: Re: Wow
item.content:encoded: hey don't pin this on me kalinin did a great job

item.title: Re: Wow
item.content:encoded: Before this happens again, I should clarify for people who aren't as familiar with Randomini's style. This wasn't Kalinin's. I was making a joke.

item.title: Wow
item.content:encoded: This…This was good. Thanks for wrtiting this Kalinin Who ever the writer is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, look at all of this wordplay and symbolism someone dropped here. This style is the very distinctive hallmark of a specific user. I could never mistake it for anyone else.

+1, Kalinin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…
Who…
Wait…
…
What?

Am I missing something?
